abstract:
Abstract
                  Human–AI collaboration is considered the most promising way to incorporate AI in the workplace. What remains unexplored are the experiential consequences of this teaming. More specifically, in a team with AI, how humans perceive themselves (self-perception) and how they are perceived by their coworkers (peer perception) in terms of work ownership and job meaningfulness. In a $2\times 2\times 2$ vignette study (n=50), participants rated perceptions of ownership, affect, job meaningfulness and satisfaction, and role dynamics across two levels (low/high) of AI proactivity and AI competency as within-subject factors, with point-of-view (self perception/peer perception) as between-subjects. Our results showed that AI with low competency or low proactivity generally improved feelings related to ownership, meaningfulness, satisfaction, and role dynamics, and also increased positive affect while reducing negative affect. However, these effects were often influenced by point-of-view. For instance, low AI proactivity resulted in higher job satisfaction from self-perception rather than peer perception. Based on our findings, we argue that designing AI for the future of work solely around performance metrics may not be adequate. Highly competent and proactive AI-driven systems can have undesirable impacts on perceptions of ownership, job identity, social image and team dynamics, and consequently, job meaningfulness.

ur.ai_reason: 研究人-AI协作团队中自我/同行感知，vignette研究虽涉及HCI但非直接用户交互
ur.tldr: 研究AI能力与主动性如何影响职场中人类的自我与同事感知。采用2×2×2 vignette实验（n=50），发现低AI能力或低主动性会提升工作所有权感、意义感、满意度及角色动力，但效果受视角（自我vs同事）调节。高能力、高主动性的AI系统可能对所有权感知、工作认同、社交形象和团队动态产生负面影响。

title: The new social image: how AI competency and AI proactivity influence self- and peer-perceptions in the workplace
title_zh: 新的社会形象：AI能力与AI主动性如何影响职场中的自我与同伴感知
